# @zakkster/lite-headless / button

Reactive `pressed` / `loading` / `disabled` states + auto-locked click
handler for async submit flows. Thin wrapper around the native
`<button>` -- it owns the three-state ARIA wiring + the gating logic
so consumers don't keep reimplementing it with subtle bugs.

## When you need this

- "Submit" with async backend → loading state + auto-disabled (prevents
  the classic double-click bug).
- Toggle button (Bold, Italic, Mute Mic) → `aria-pressed` paint.
- Confirm-vote in Twitch Prediction → locks during request.

For plain non-stateful buttons, use a native `<button>` directly; you
don't need a primitive.

## Quick example

```js
import { createButton } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/button";

// Action button with async submit:
const btn = createButton({
    onPress: () => submitVote(),    // can return a Promise
});
btn.attachRoot(buttonEl);

// Or call runAsync explicitly:
btn.runAsync(async () => {
    await submitVote();
});

// Toggle button:
const bold = createButton({ toggle: true, pressed: false });
bold.attachRoot(boldButtonEl);
bold.setPressed(true);    // aria-pressed becomes "true"
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `toggle` | `false` | If true (or if `pressed` is a boolean), button is a toggle (paints `aria-pressed`). Click flips pressed automatically. |
| `pressed` | `false` | Initial pressed state. Setting this implies `toggle: true`. |
| `loading` | `false` | Initial loading state. |
| `disabled` | `false` | Initial disabled state. |
| `onPress` | - | `(ev) => any \| Promise<any>`. If it returns a Promise, the button auto-locks during the await (same as `runAsync`). |

## API

```ts
{
    // Accessors (reactive)
    isPressed(): boolean
    isLoading(): boolean
    isDisabled(): boolean
    canPress(): boolean    // true iff a click would be honored
    toggle: boolean         // true if this is a toggle button

    // Mutations
    setPressed(b: boolean): void     // no-op if not a toggle button
    setLoading(b: boolean): void
    setDisabled(b: boolean): void
    runAsync<T>(fn: () => Promise<T> | T): Promise<T | undefined>

    // Attach
    attachRoot(buttonEl): off

    // Lifecycle
    destroy(): void
}
```

## Gating

`canPress()` returns `true` iff:
- not disabled
- not loading
- not destroyed

When `canPress()` is false:
- The click event is intercepted (`preventDefault` + `stopImmediatePropagation`).
  Even JS-dispatched clicks (`el.click()`) are blocked, which native
  `disabled` would not protect against.
- `onPress` is not called.
- `runAsync` returns `undefined` without invoking its `fn`.

## Painted attributes (root)

- `data-button-root` — slot marker
- `data-pressed` — boolean; mirrors `isPressed()` (only meaningful for toggle buttons)
- `data-loading` — boolean
- `data-disabled` — boolean
- `aria-pressed="true"|"false"` — painted on toggle buttons; literal string per ARIA spec
- `aria-busy="true"` — painted during loading
- `disabled` — native DOM attribute; set when either explicitly disabled OR loading

Note on `disabled` ownership: the primitive sets `disabled` based on
its internal state. If your consumer adds a `disabled` attribute
externally (outside of `setDisabled`), the primitive won't see it as
its own state -- the effect will overwrite it on the next paint.
Always go through `setDisabled` or `host.setDisabled` on the wrapper.

## Wrapper (`<lite-button>`)

```html
<lite-button id="submit-bet">Submit Bet</lite-button>

<lite-button toggle pressed>Bold</lite-button>

<lite-button loading>Submitting…</lite-button>
```

Attributes (all reactive):
- `toggle` — boolean; makes it a toggle button
- `pressed` — boolean (toggle only)
- `loading` — boolean
- `disabled` — boolean

If the host is not a `<button>`, the wrapper adds `role="button"` and
`tabindex="0"`. Strongly prefer a real `<button>` -- it gets you
keyboard activation (Space/Enter) + form integration for free.

Host methods:
- `host.setPressed(b)`, `host.setLoading(b)`, `host.setDisabled(b)`
- `host.runAsync(fn)` — convenience pass-through
- `host._buttonInstance` (advanced)

Host accessors:
- `host.isPressed`, `host.isLoading`, `host.canPress`
- `host.isDisabledState` — named to avoid clobbering the native
  `disabled` DOM property reflection

Event: `press` — `{ detail: { event } }` — fires when the button is
activated (mouse, keyboard, or programmatic `el.click()`).

## Composition

### Twitch Prediction "Submit" pattern

```html
<lite-button id="submit" disabled>
    <span data-button-label>Submit</span>
    <span data-button-spinner aria-hidden="true">⟳</span>
</lite-button>
```

```js
const host = document.getElementById("submit");
// Enable once a radio in the Prediction radio-group is checked:
predictionRG.addEventListener("valuechange", (e) => {
    host.setDisabled(e.detail.value == null);
});
host.addEventListener("press", async () => {
    await host.runAsync(() => api.submitVote(predictionRG.value));
});
```

```css
[data-button-spinner] { display: none; }
[data-loading] [data-button-spinner] {
    display: inline-block;
    animation: spin 1s linear infinite;
}
[data-loading] [data-button-label] { opacity: 0.6; }
```

## CSS contract

Follows `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. `data-pressed`/`data-loading`/`data-disabled`
are Class-2 booleans. `data-button-root` is a Class-4 slot marker.
